div.p: Output Range: (-1, +1) Formula: tanh(x) = (eˣ - e⁻ˣ)/(eˣ + e⁻ˣ) Derivative: d/dx tanh(x) = 1 - tanh²(x) Properties:

ul.li: Good for hidden layers in small networks

div.p: Output Range: [0, +∞) Formula: ReLU(x) = max(0, x) Derivative: d/dx ReLU(x) = 1 if x > 0, else 0 Properties:
Output Range: (-∞, +∞) Formula: f(x) = x Derivative: d/dx f(x) = 1 Properties:

ul.li: Used for output layer in regression

div.p: Without activation functions, a multi-layer network collapses to a single linear transformation:
W₂(W₁x + b₁) + b₂ = W'x + b' (just another linear layer!)
Activation functions "break" this collapse, allowing networks to approximate any continuous function (Universal Approximation Theorem).
